calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-14-298199 The Milton and Mary Krieger Revocable Living Trust and PCN-298200 Conservatorship of Mary Krieger.

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Francisco Fabrega (Conservator/Trustee).
Sheila Robello Court appointed Counsel for Conservatee (now deceased).

Ms. Callejo stated that she will be petitioning to terminate supervision of the trust, that there are no current disputes, that there are two beneficiaries and that the house will have to be sold. Ms. Callejo also stated that as of the accounting there was $5,000.00 in the conservatorship account, that as of February of last year there was $120,000.00 in the trust account, that she is not sure how much is in the account now, that she believes her Client can just transfer the money and that she believes there is a poor over will. In addition, Ms. Callejo stated that she will check with her Client, that the account is held by her Client and that they can do a 13100. The Court stated that there will need to be a final document filed in the conservatorship matter. Ms. Callejo stated that she will determine how the funds are to be disposed of either to the trust or to the beneficiaries, that there are no creditors, that the conservatorship will end solvent, that the only income in the conservatorship was the Conservatee's social security benefits, spoke of work performed and stated that all third party caregivers have been paid/taken care of. Ms. Robello spoke of the Conservatee's preference to have Maria Fabrega as her caregiver. The Court granted the Second Account; Petition for Attorney Fees and Costs; for Reduction of Bond, and to Dispense with Future Accountings in the conservatorship matter and the Second Account; Petition for Trustee Fees, for Attorney Fees and Costs, and for Reduction of Bond in the trust matter (subject to review of the orders). Status on filing of a final petition in the conservatorship matter is set for April 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-14-298199 The Milton and Mary Krieger Revocable Living Trust and PCN-14-298200 Conservatorship of Mary Krieger.

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Francisco Fabrega.

Ms. Callejo requested a two to three week continuance stating that she received both accountings from the Bookkeeper, that she will be working on the papers and that she is dealing with a family medical issue. Status on filing of the second accountings is continued to May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-04-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-14-198199 The Milton & Mary Krieger Revocable trust and PCN-14-298200 Conservatorship of Mary Krieger.
Appearances: Francesca Callejo for (trustee/conservator) Francisco Fabrega. Court Appointed Counsel Sheila Robello for Mary Krieger. Maria Fabrega also present.

Both counsel confirmed that they did not receive examiner's notes. Ms. Callejo reported that she received the verified statements on Friday, that they were e-filed and that she has retained the originals, that she can deliver a copy if necessary, that the notice for the continued date was e-filed but she did not bring a copy and that the draws on the loan were deposited into the conservatorship and paid out. The Court asked about the relationship between Mr. Haigh and the conservator. Ms. Callejo explained that they were business associates, that Mr. Haigh helped with the accounting and that they are friendly but that it is a business relationship. The Court asked about the request to pay Maria and that she had previously stated that she did not want pay. Ms. Callejo stated that they had suggested that she should be paid if she wants and that it is in the amended petition. The Court stated that regarding the request for the conservatee's right to vote that it will need to be a separate petition to be heard on a Thursday and that regarding the trust there is an issue regarding two deposits of $350.00 each on 2/13/15 & 3/6/15 which are not listed. Ms. Callejo stated that she is not sure. The Court asked about the sale on 11/25/15 of Goldman Sachs and that there needs to be clarification on the number of shares. Ms. Callejo stated that all expenditures were paid out of the conservatorship account, that if the court prefers everything can go to the trust, that all bills are being kept current, that Mr. Fabrega visited a couple of days ago, that he was able to go to Morgan Stanley to pick up statements that they would not release to Ms. Callejo, that they will have to file another petition and that they were trying to conserve costs, that the only asset in the trust is the house and that Mr. Fabrega has no authority to sell it. Ms. Robello stated that provided that the funds are bonded and accounted for she has no issues, that payment to Ms. Fabrega of $700.00 per month is modest and that the conservatee and Ms. Fabrega enjoy going out. Ms. Fabrega thanked the Court stating that medical care had been an issue, that the court is now holding her brother accountable and that she is grateful for the assistance of Ms. Robello. The Court took the accounting petitions in each case under submission as of October 24, 2016 stating that it will have the examiner send out notes and that it expects responses to the notes to be filed no later than October 17, 2016. Ms. Callejo stated that it was best to receive the notes via email and that she will email Ms. Hilton with the information.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Bring The Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Continued To Jun-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francesca Callejo for Francisco Fabrega (successor trustee). Sheila Robello for Mary Krieger (Conservatee)

Ms. Callejo stated that her client has been channeling all of the income into the conservator account, suggested that the trust may not have to be under court supervision, asked for guidance on how much the bond should be increased to, stated that her client has waived compensation and reported that Mary is recovering well from her stroke. Furth more Ms. Callejo informed the Court that she is happy to run the numbers by Ms. Robello and will file an ex parte application to increase the bond.

Ms. Robello reported that the Trustee has moved out of state and is unsure if there is adequate jurisdiction, that she is ok with the assets bonded in the conservatorship, reported that Mary is doing well at home and stated that all that is left in the trust is the real property.

The Court continued the status on filing a petition to bring the trust under court supervision to June 27, 2016 at 9:00a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Bring The Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francesca Callejo for (trustee) Francisco Fabrega. Court appointed counsel Sheila Robello for Mary Krieger.

The Court stated that the examiner was to have notified the parties to bring the trust under court supervision. Ms. Callejo stated that she did not receive specific instructions, that her and Ms. Robello have been talking about the current necessity of it, that the trust had limited liquid assets until the reverse mortgage, that they will be filing an accounting in the conservatorship case and request a 6 week continuance to finalize that, that Ms. Robello was concerned that Mr. Fabrega returned to Oregon, that the trust is straightforward, and that they have agreed on the continuance to work something out. Ms. Robello reported that Mr. Fabrega was named co-trustee with his sister, that she has resigned, that he has moved out of state, that it looks like the reverse mortgage proceeds are in the conservatorship account which could be bonded, that they have been given copies of the statements for review, that they would like to wait for the conservatorship accounting and can submit a written report to the court prior to the next hearing. Ms. Callejo stated that technically the conservatorship accounting is not yet due, that Mr. Fabrega was appointed permanently 5 months ago but had been the temporary conservator for a year before that, that the accounting is not due for several months but they will do it early, that they are trying to keep expenses to a minimum and that Ms. Krieger is in good health and funds are limited. Ms. Robello clarified that she has requested to bring the trust under court supervision because the trustee had moved out of state but that she has been further informed and this may not be necessary. Ms. Callejo stated that they will prepare the conservatorship accounting, that Ms. Robello will review it and let the court know if she thinks this should go forward, that Mr. Fabrega moving back to Oregon was a benefit and things are now calmer at the conservatee's home. The status on filing a petition to bring the trust under court supervision was continued to May 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

Petition Of Successor Co-Trustee And Beneficiary To Remove Successor Co-Trustee Maria Ana Fabrega And Confirm Petitioner As Sole Successor Trustee Or
Off Calendar Nov-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francesca Callejo for and with (petitioner) Francisco Fabrega. Court appointed counsel Sheila Robello for Mary Krieger.

Ms. Callejo reported that Maria Fabrega signed a resignation but it has not been filed yet, that lenders are cautious regarding trustees entering into RAM's, that there is a financial need as the conservatorship income is not sufficient, that the lender is clear about what is to be/not be in the order, that Ms. Robello has an updated statement and that they need an order today authorizing the trustee. The Court stated that it needs to know what the RAM is. Ms. Callejo explained the formula of the RAM and offered to either file the document or attach it to the order. The Court asked if Maria Fabrega has waived bond and that confirmation will require either a bond or a waiver. Ms. Callejo stated that she had not asked Ms. Fabrega to waive bond but did not believe that her resignation would result in the necessity of a bond. Ms. Robello stated that the provisions are problematic, that the parties agreed that Francisco would handle things and are looking to formalize that, that he has been handling the trust for some time and that they are only here due to the requirement for the RAM. Ms. Callejo stated that the sole objective is to keep the conservatee in her home. The Court stated that it will take the petition to remove co-trustee and confirm petitioner as sole trustee under submission subject to receiving a supplement explaining the terms of the RAM, a revised order and a statement from Maria filed stating that she consents to Francisco serving without bond. Ms. Callejo stated that she will submit a declaration with the terms of the loan. The Court stated that it takes a dim view of bank lawyers dictating to it what is or is not in an order and that the court will need to be informed if Maria will not sign the statement.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
